channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: EU Plans More Flexibility for Industry in Carbon Overhaul
item.description: The European Union aims to give its ailing industrial sector more flexibility in an upcoming reform of the bloc’s flagship carbon market, while ensuring companies boost investment in clean technologies.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska

item.title: Private Equity Funds Seek Climate Experts as Risks Rise
item.description: Investors are turning to specialist tools and companies to understand escalating threats from extreme weather events.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee, Mary Hui, Cao Ban

item.title: London Moves to Expand Influence in $140 Billion ILS Market
item.description: The world’s biggest hub for commercial and specialty risk is angling for a much more prominent role in the $140 billion global market for insurance-linked securities.
item.dc:creator: Gautam Naik

item.title: Heat Wave Threatens Output at Five French Nuclear Reactors
item.description: France’s latest heat wave is straining the country’s power system, with Electricite de France SA warning that as many as five nuclear plants could face output curbs in the coming days as soaring temperatures affect rivers used for cooling.
item.dc:creator: Will Mathis, Joe Wertz

item.title: Private Equity Turns to Heat Detectives as Climate Risks Intensify
item.description: For decades, investment models at the largest private equity funds relied on assumptions of relatively stable weather patterns. Now frequent extreme events are creating new threats for their portfolio companies.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee

item.title: Nuclear Power to Climb 44% Worldwide as China Tops US, BNEF Says
item.description: Global nuclear capacity is set to climb 44% over the next decade after years of tepid growth, spurred by growing demand for electricity and aggressive efforts to build reactors in China and India.
item.dc:creator: Will Wade

item.title: Milan’s Battle Against Bad Air Goes On
item.description: A quirk of geography has hampered the Italian city’s ongoing efforts to reduce pollution from vehicle emissions and other regional sources.
item.dc:creator: Autor não encontrado

item.title: Tencent Inks First Deal for Carbon-Removal Credits Outside China
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd will buy 300,000 carbon removal credits from an agroforestry project in Indonesia, marking the first deal of its kind for the Chinese technology firm outside its domestic market.
item.dc:creator: Alastair Marsh

item.title: China’s Deadly Storms Turbocharged by Convergence of Factors
item.description: This week’s deadly storms in southern and central China were worsened by a convergence of factors, underscoring the growing frequency of weather extremes that the world’s second-largest economy must increasingly contend with.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui

item.title: Coffee Trader Seeks to Shield More Vietnam Growers From Weather
item.description: Major coffee trader ECOM Agroindustrial Corp. is pushing for a fivefold increase in the number of growers covered by its specialty weather insurance in Vietnam, where the industry depends on thousands of smallholder farmers increasingly exposed to climate risk.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui, Cao Ban
